editorialSummary.text: Sleek property with a rooftop pool & restaurant, plus a spa with steam rooms & saunas.

text.text:
From the moment I stepped into the hotel, I was deeply moved by the warm hospitality. I especially remember the gentleman at the entrance who expressed his love for Korea and shared a wonderful conversation about Korean food. His friendliness made me feel right at home from the very beginning.

As an Accor Platinum member, I was incredibly grateful for the Bosphorus View room upgrade, and the scenery was simply spectacular. It was also a wonderful opportunity to experience the special Ramadan buffet curated by the hotel. The Half-Board (Breakfast & Dinner) meals were of excellent quality, and the traditional ritual dance during dinner was a mesmerizing and unforgettable experience.

However, I must mention a disappointing incident during a busy weekend breakfast. We were assigned a table, but another guest was mistakenly seated there. The staff were unaware of the double-booking until I brought it to their attention, and even then, we were left standing with our food for quite some time without a proper apology. This was quite unexpected for a 5-star hotel of this caliber, especially considering how much I enjoyed the rest of the services.

Despite this, the individual staff members truly make this hotel special. The Lounge staff were consistently kind and attentive throughout our stay. My sincere thanks to Chef Mehmet Efir for his thoughtful care every morning, and Zeynepnur at the reception, who assisted us so kindly and professionally during our checkout.

Swissôtel The Bosphorus made my final days in Istanbul truly perfect. I highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting the city and look forward to returning soon.

text.text: It’s our first time in Istanbul and also first time staying at the Swissotel. The hotel is beautiful and service has been incredible. We loved the Executive Lounge’s food and drinks and most importantly our waitress Öznur. She was so wonderful, attentive, sweet and welcoming. Totally made our first evening and looking forward to more snacks and drinks up there because of her. Would 100% recommend to anyone! 😊

text.text:
My wife and I stayed at the Swissotel in early February, and it was, without question, the finest hotel experience we have ever had. Every detail, from the exceptional hospitality and outstanding meals to the immaculate room, thoughtful gifts, and true five-star service, made our stay unforgettable. We strongly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Istanbul. From the moment we arrived, we were treated like family, and the consistent care from the staff made our visit especially meaningful. We would like to extend special thanks to Asli, Busra, and Nuray.

Asli warmly introduced us to the hotel team, provided a wonderful tour of the property, arranged complimentary desserts and coffee, and recommended outstanding dining locations, including Madhu’s and the 16 Roof Bar. She also went above and beyond by helping my wife access a hotel computer to manage critical graduate school scheduling. Her generosity and professionalism truly represent the gold standard of service.

Busra made every breakfast memorable. Each day she ensured we were well cared for, offering attentive service, friendly conversation, and helpful recommendations for must-see spots around Istanbul. She even organized a romantic pancake breakfast on our final morning. Her dedication, warmth, and exceptional work ethic made a lasting impression, and we are deeply grateful for her efforts.

Nuray was equally outstanding. She ensured every aspect of our stay met the highest standard and thoughtfully arranged a VIP visit to the executive lounge, where we enjoyed coffee with a breathtaking view of downtown Istanbul. Words cannot fully express our appreciation for her kindness and hospitality.

Do not miss the opportunity to stay at the Swissotel. We promise you it is absolutely worth it!

text.text:
I sincerely adored my stay and was genuinely disappointed to leave so soon. I truly wished I could have stayed longer. The room was impeccable, the cleanliness outstanding, and the service consistently excellent despite the hotel being fully packed and hosting VIP events. Even the marketing team, Zeineb and Ipek, embody a rare and exceptional attitude: “no problem we can’t solve, and we’ll go to any length to make you happy.” This is the true ethos of hospitality and exactly why guests choose one hotel over another, even within the same brand and group.

Their generosity shines through in every detail. The lady concierge managed to find things even ChatGPT couldn’t track down, which speaks volumes about her resourcefulness. As for the wonderful hostess manager, Lauretta, she makes you feel instantly at home, as if you were an old friend stopping by for a visit.

All of this reflects the strength of the general manager Utkan Gulacti leading the property.

In my opinion, a hotel represents not only its brand but also the team that brings it to life, and here, the entire team feels like a group of true stars. They deserve sincere applause. I wholeheartedly commend them for their attention to details, their quick response despite being extremely busy,  please keep  up the good work, and honestly, my next stay can only be in this hotel. They made leaving incredibly difficult and left me wishing I had spent my entire time there. I hope to see them soon.

text.text:
It is one of the loveliest restaurant in Istanbul - we visit it every time when we come to Istanbul.

I would like to start from booking of tables - usually we come, book it in their special waiting list and about 1 hour later we get it.
Last time we came about 1:30 pm and went right away.

About food - we tried a lot from menu and like all of the dishes.

About price - I suppose with this amazing views and very on Bosphorus and nice interior, atmosphere inside of restaurant, the prices are very comfortable and normal.

No taxes under price - it is also very nice.

About waiters - they are kind, smiling. But don’t speak English. One manager helped us with menu - it was very pleased.

And about payment - only card or Istanbul card.

It was great experience and we will come back in future more and more. Love this restaurant so much!!!

editorialSummary.text: Pizzas, international cuisine & shisha in a modern alcohol-free restaurant with Bosphorus views.

text.text:
Good for Privacy, but Priced at a Premium

We visited the VIP lounge at Huqqa with friends. The lounge access starts at $150, which includes a basic welcome plate of fruits, chocolates, and mixed nuts. Be aware that everything else—including hookah, any food orders (we tried the pasta and chicken sandwich), and even coffee—is an additional charge.

While the service was excellent and the staff were very friendly, the final bill can quickly add up. We ended up paying around $300 for what felt like very little food. The lounge is a great choice if you value privacy above all else, but be prepared for the steep pricing.

We had a really great time with our friends. We ordered the gum mint hookah, tea, and the Gayser Cikolata (like a hot brownie with ice cream on top and melted marshmallow inside). It was a new taste for me, and I really enjoyed it!

If you arrive earlier, you can sit at the tables with an outdoor view, which is really beautiful. The outdoor environment overall was very nice (I shared some pictures).

The noise level is loud, but you can still have a conversation without too much trouble.

The service was good, though note that there’s a 13% service charge added to the bill.

text.text:
A creative and vibrant cultural hub in the heart of Istanbul.
Yapı Kredi bomontiada is one of those places where art, music, and social life meet perfectly. I visited during an exhibition at the Ara Güler Museum and loved the inspiring atmosphere industrial architecture mixed with a modern artistic spirit. The courtyard is lively, surrounded by cafes and restaurants where you can relax after exploring the exhibits. It’s a great spot for art lovers, photographers, and anyone who enjoys spending time in a creative environment. Truly one of Istanbul’s cultural gems.

We had an excellent breakfast at the Fairmont Hotel restaurant. The service and atmosphere were wonderful from the moment we arrived. Burcu, the hostess, greeted us warmly and made us feel very welcome.
A special thank you to Tanju, the Breakfast Manager, who runs a fantastic service. Everything was well organized and the staff were very attentive. Chef Koray also deserves recognition, the food was fresh, delicious, and beautifully prepared.

One of our favourite parts was the special Turkish coffee served with crepe. It was absolutely delicious and a perfect way to finish breakfast. We really enjoyed the experience and would happily come back again.

text.text:
I would like to express my sincere appreciation for the outstanding service I have received during my stay at the Fairmont Istanbul.

From my very first morning in the dining commons, three individuals — Tanju, Baran and Burcu have consistently gone above and beyond in ways that truly elevate the guest experience.

Baran , who has been hosting my table each morning, brings an exceptional level of attentiveness and genuine warmth. He checks in regularly, remembers my preferences, engages in light and enjoyable conversation, and makes thoughtful recommendations. One morning he even brought me a special crêpe to try, which was such a memorable and personal touch.

Tanju, the restaurant manager, exemplifies leadership through presence and care. He personally introduced me to a house-made juice that he recommended — easily a 10 out of 10 in flavor and presentation. He consistently comes by the table to ensure everything is perfect and always takes the initiative to make sure I have absolutely everything I need.

Burcu greets me each morning with a warm smile and positive energy that truly sets the tone for the day. Whether welcoming me in or offering a ginger shot or complimentary tea as I leave, she brings a level of hospitality that feels both refined and heartfelt.

I travel frequently and have stayed in many high-end properties, including Dubai, and the Four Seasons in China.Without hesitation, I can say that the service and hospitality I have experienced here at the Fairmont Istanbul ranks as the very best I have received anywhere in the world.

What makes any organization truly exceptional is its people — their energy, their competence, and the pride they bring to their roles. Michael, Adam, and Tiffany embody those qualities beautifully. Because of them, and the overall quality of the Fairmont experience, I will absolutely be returning in May for a longer stay after my current two-week visit concludes.

Please extend my gratitude to them. They have made this trip even more memorable and I plan on returning in May because of the staff at this hotel. .

text.text:
This is my fav hotel in Ist. Its price perf ratio is great and it offers very good service in general. Strongly rec.

1- Two restaurants the gastro TR Aila and the Japanese Izumo are simply great. Don't miss these gems.
2- The buffet rest has a serious service problem.
3- Room service is really good, offering tasty food.
Overall food quality is great - likely outperforming 95+ % of 5* hotels in Ist.
4- The build quality and materials are also very good.
5- Has a very nice lounge
What I like most is that it's low key luxury.

text.text:
Fantastic hotel in a great location. We stayed here after aesthetic surgery and there was even a pillow menu and option to request ice packs, as well as a variety of in-room entertainment options like Apple TV to keep us occupied whilst we couldn’t go out.

The upgrade to a Gold room was well worth it for access to the lounge and butler service, who helped us pack post-surgery.

Ali Oğuz (Concierge) and Seven Altay (Gold Lounge Manager) were absolutely amazing and their encyclopaedic knowledge and extensive list of local contacts helped us with everything from restaurants, hairdressers, local tailors and shops to wedding venue hire…and they had contacts in each of the places suggested that were able to help us also. They completely changed our trip for the better with their help and advice. Irem in the butler team was also very helpful as was Baran.

text.text:
I visited Istanbul from Mexico, and one of the places on my list was Mahir, a true classic of Turkish cuisine that I definitely recommend.
Prices are a bit higher compared to other Turkish restaurants, but it is absolutely worth it for the quality.

The place is very clean and there is usually a line to get in, which says a lot about its popularity. The waiters are fast and friendly, and at this location there is always a woman (apparently the manager idk) closely supervising everything to make sure the service runs smoothly. She is also very attentive and helpful with customers.

I traveled from far away just to try the lahmacun, and it was without a doubt the best I had in all of Turkey. The rest of the dishes are also very good.
They accept card payments, the menu is varied, and the restaurant is consistently clean. Definitely a place worth visiting.

text.text:
I was intrigued by the endless queues. Every branch always seems busy, with eager customers waiting patiently in the cold, rain, or sunshine. Curious, I decided to join them and went in as hungry as I could be.

I ordered a full spread: lahmacun, soup, hot dishes, kebabs, salad, and rice. I went all in. Unfortunately, I left hungry.

To be clear, portion sizes are generous, but the flavour was missing. I only managed a few spoonfuls of each dish, hoping the next one would be better, but it never quite delivered.

text.text: Now if you want ridiculously delicious Turkish cuisine, this is the place. It felt like someone grandma whos had a passion for cooking for over 50 years prepared the food. There's always a queue outside so I suggest avoiding coming during peak hours. The food here was so good I had to come back the next day and have my last meal here before heading to the airport. I recommend the stuffed balls(unbelievable), the pide is amazing, the adana too... the lamb shank(incik) is ridiculous, and the Lahmacun is to die for! I will see you guys soon as Im back in Istanbul please do NOT change the recipes because all the food here is fantastic!! Oh and its very affordable dare I say cheap for the level of quality of the food you get. 5 stars!

text.text:
We tried the lamb neck and found it quite disappointing — it tasted like it was seasoned with only salt and pepper and lacked real flavor.

The joojeh kebab was also underwhelming, as some pieces weren’t cooked all the way through.

On a positive note, the ayran and mast moosir were really good and definitely the highlight of the meal. The staff were polite.

Overall, I wouldn’t come back or recommend this restaurant.

Their prices are reasonable and reflect the quality offered. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy genuine Persian cuisine.

text.text:
Reihana Restaurant in Istanbul is a good choice if you’re looking for Iranian food.
The food is generally good, and the chicken is tasty. We ordered chicken with rice and Shirazi salad.

The rice quality is average; it would be better to use a higher-quality rice that suits Iranian cuisine.
The chicken is good, but it could use a little more seasoning, such as some salt and a touch of pepper.

The atmosphere is nice, and thank you to Salih Eser for the good service

text.text:
The place is amazing! I went there on Saturday, and surprisingly it wasn’t crowded, we even got a table right away. I ordered Kaşarlı Köfte Izgara, basically grilled meat with cheese. The portion was large, and the price was affordable. I also tried my friend’s Mantar Kavurma, which was absolutely delicious, mushrooms with small pearl onions.

Lastly, we ordered profiterole. The portion was generous, not too sweet, and very reasonably priced. The service was good, and the waitresses were very helpful. On top of that, the place has a nice view, you can even see a small part of the Bosphorus. Would go back here!

I love İBB restaurants, and most of the time I prefer these municipality places.
İBB Fatih Paşa Sosyal Tesisleri is located in the heart of the forest, surrounded by the calm of nature. It’s a beautiful restaurant in an old building that looks like a small palace — the perfect spot to enjoy delicious food at great prices.

Although the çoban salad didn’t have onions, I can easily forgive that 🤗
Some comments mention it’s very crowded and you might have to wait, but we were lucky to get a table right away.
We’ll definitely come back again!

text.text:
Super nice and delicious restaurant! Worth the money spent!

In a top spot, by the Bosphorus, you can admire the ocean traffic and the seagulls fishing. Great views all around you!

The food was tasty and prepared with culinary skills. Drinks were also quite special.

Prior reservations are recommended.

text.text:
I’ll be trying more dishes soon, but so far — the food is definitely tasty. We mostly ordered starters, and I’d rate them 8–9 out of 10. Really solid. As for the mains and the service: the service was very good. The staff were friendly and welcoming. They even offered to move us to a better table with a Bosphorus view as soon as one became available — that was a very nice touch.

Overall, everything was great — but not quite a wow experience. That’s the only reason I’m giving it 4 stars instead of 5. Not because something was missing, but simply because everything was good, just not exceptional.

The hot dishes were a bit weaker compared to the starters — we ordered two mains. Still, the overall experience was pleasant and I enjoyed it.

text.text:
Difficult Reach

Location: The restaurant is situated in a beautiful area, but unfortunately, it’s quite difficult to access. Reaching the venue can be challenging, especially for first-time visitors.

Car Parking: No dedicated parking is available, which adds to the inconvenience of visiting, particularly if you’re arriving by car.

Entrance: The main entrance is hard to find and unwelcoming, with a closed gate that can confuse guests and detract from the overall first impression.

Customer Service: The staff were polite, but not very attentive to customers’ needs. Requests took time to be fulfilled, and there was little initiative in offering assistance or recommendations.

Staff: Courteous but not very proactive or engaging, especially considering the high-end expectations of the venue.

Dining Tables: The tables are comfortable, but most do not offer the expected scenic Bosphorus view, which is one of the main attractions advertised by the restaurant.

Food Menu: The food is the highlight of the experience. The Albanian liver and Rack of Lamb were both perfectly cooked and full of flavor—definitely worth trying.

Hygiene: Cleanliness is well maintained throughout the dining area and facilities.

Drinks: Unfortunately, the drinks did not meet expectations in terms of taste or variety.

Prices: The restaurant is very expensive, and given the service and experience, it doesn’t fully justify the price tag.

Overall: Feriye Restaurant offers great food in a classy setting, but logistical difficulties, lack of parking, and service gaps make it fall short of a premium dining experience. Suitable for a special culinary try, but not for those seeking ease and full value.

text.text:
We visited this place for breakfast during our trip to Turkey, and it truly exceeded our expectations. The food was outstanding — fresh, high quality, and full of authentic flavors that made us want to try everything on the table.
The service made the experience even better. Sefa was incredibly kind, professional, and attentive to every detail, making sure we had everything we needed. He made us feel genuinely welcome from the very first moment.
I highly recommend this place. If you are in the city, it is definitely worth visiting — it’s not just breakfast, it’s a whole experience.

text.text:
had an absolutely amazing experience at Bazlama Kahvaltı Nişantaşı!
Their breakfast was incredibly delicious every single item was fresh, full of flavor, and beautifully presented. The quality of the food was outstanding, and the service was beyond excellent  honestly, I’ve never experienced such a warm and professional service before.

All the fruits, cheeses, and ingredients they used were perfectly fresh and high-quality. We truly enjoyed every moment and could have easily spent hours there chatting and relaxing with friends.

A huge thank you to the management and the whole team for creating such a wonderful concept and atmosphere  it really makes every customer feel happy and satisfied.

Even after breakfast, I ordered a few takeaway boxes to bring home, and they were beautifully designed and carefully packed  everything was neatly separated and fresh. My friends absolutely loved them too! The service was exceptional, and every single dish was just perfect.

I’ll definitely come back next time I’m in Istanbul, and I highly recommend this place to everyone! ❤️

The 'unlimited' concept is fantastic—you pay a set price (around 1000 - 1200 TL)and they keep bringing fresh bazlama, gözleme, and pişi to your table. The variety of homemade jams is incridible ! text.text: For me, Beşiktaş is the most vibrant and lively center of Istanbul. It’s the place where I used to hang out with older friends before matches and where the walk to İnönü Stadium began on game days. In Beşiktaş Çarşı, you can find all kinds of restaurants, shops, fishmongers, and fruit vendors. The nightlife continues as if it were daytime. It’s a beautiful center where you can have a great time, one of the best spots in Istanbul.

text.text:
Ruby in Ortaköy is, without doubt, the No.1 place in Istanbul.
We were truly impressed by the quality of the service, the kindness and positive energy, and of course the excellent food. The service is at the highest level, professional and genuinely warm, making you feel welcome from the very first moment.

What makes Ruby truly special is also the smooth transformation from a classy, elegant restaurant into a vibrant and stylish club one level below. The transition feels natural and sophisticated.
All of this comes with breathtaking views towards the Bosphorus Bridge and the iconic Ortaköy Mosque.

An outstanding venue that perfectly combines gastronomy, atmosphere and nightlife.

text.text:
I’ve been to Ruby Club in Istanbul twice, and I really enjoyed both times! The vibe is stylish and energetic  it’s not a huge place, but it has two floors and a cute little balcony area that gives it a unique charm.

The music was amazing on both nights, and the dancers were beautiful and added to the whole atmosphere. The staff were very kind and the service was also really good.

But the one thing that bothered me was how strict they were at the entrance about the dress code. They really insist that you wear high heels and dress up. But the last time I was there, I saw many girls inside wearing sneakers and even cargo-style pants 😅  so the rules didn’t seem consistent at all.

Overall though, it’s a fun and stylish club with great energy. Just be ready to dress up in case they’re strict that night!

text.text:
We stayed at Raffles Istanbul with my wife and everything was exceptional from start to finish — from the welcome at the entrance, to the room, to breakfast. Service was warm, polished, and genuinely attentive, and the room was spotless, quiet, and beautifully designed. Breakfast was excellent with great quality and variety, and overall dining throughout the hotel was consistently strong. The location is also perfect.

One highlight deserves a special mention: sushi at Isokyo was honestly the best we’ve had in Türkiye. The balance of flavors, the presentation, and the ambiance were all on another level.

If you want a premium Istanbul stay where everything flows smoothly and the standards are truly high, this is an easy recommendation.

text.text:
We stayed at this hotel for five days to surprise my wife for our anniversary, and it was truly a perfect experience from start to finish.

Every aspect of the hotel exceeded our expectations. We tried the breakfast, the brunch, the restaurant, as well as the massage and spa. Everything was absolutely perfect. The quality of service, the atmosphere, and the attention to detail made our celebration even more special.

A very special thank you to our wonderful butler, Aliza, who took exceptional care of us throughout our stay. The butler team prepared thoughtful surprises for us every single day, which made our anniversary celebration even more memorable. We truly felt pampered and valued as guests.

We are beyond satisfied with our experience and are already planning our next stay. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a special and unforgettable getaway!

text.text:
This late-night spot is a real gem, especially after a long night of partying! I’ve been here a few times, and it’s become one of my go-to places when everything else is closed.

They serve sea shells in three different flavors, each one with a unique twist — it was fun to try them all and compare. They also offer fried items and cow liver rolls, which are very traditional dishes, but presented in a modern, fast-food style. It’s an interesting fusion of old and new.

The whole experience was fun, casual, and tasty — a perfect stop if you’re looking for something different and flavorful late at night. Definitely a spot I’ll keep coming back to when I want something quick, cultural, and satisfying.

text.text:
Stepping into Hafız Mustafa 1864 in Nişantaşı feels like entering a temple of tradition, flavor, and hospitality. From the moment you walk in, you're greeted by a warm, courteous staff whose genuine kindness elevates the entire experience. Their attentiveness and charm make you feel more like a guest than a customer.

Baklava & Pastries Beyond Compare The quality of the baklava is simply outstanding—flaky, golden layers of perfection, generously filled with pistachios or walnuts, and delicately soaked in syrup that strikes the ideal balance between sweet and aromatic. Every pastry, from the classic künefe to the creamy sütlaç, is a masterclass in Turkish dessert craftsmanship.

Tea & Treats, the Perfect Pairing If you choose to sit in, you're in for a treat. Their tea service is impeccable—fragrant, strong, and served piping hot, perfectly complementing the rich variety of sweets. It’s a moment of indulgence that feels both luxurious and comforting.

Take a Taste of Turkey Home Whether you're a local or a visitor, Hafız Mustafa is the go-to destination for traditional Turkish sweets to take home. Their packaging is elegant and travel-friendly, making it an ideal gift—or a personal stash of joy.

Highly Recommended This isn’t just a pastry shop—it’s a cultural experience wrapped in sugar and sincerity. Hafız Mustafa 1864 Nişantaşı is a must-visit for anyone who appreciates quality, tradition, and heartfelt service.

Hafiz Mustafa never fails to amaze me with their quality, without a doubt the best Turkish dessert and coffee shop. It is the best shop if you are looking for quality Turkish coffee and dessert, also good place to get some gifts for loved ones I was surprised to see that they sell honey and honey hives. You could also buy Turkish coffee making set here as well as the coffee itself.

We had Kunafa, tea and coffee, this is the third day in the row 😅 maybe the only thing that isn’t the best is gaining weight 😅

Would definitely recommend this place and I’ll for sure be coming back!

text.text:
We had a truly wonderful experience at this breakfast place in Istanbul.
The staff were incredibly kind, welcoming, and very helpful. We especially want to mention how they helped us bring my mother into the restaurant with her wheelchair despite the stairs — something that is absolutely not taken for granted and meant a lot to us.

The food was excellent: very diverse, beautifully presented, and made with fresh, high-quality ingredients.

My wife asked for fresh mint, and although they didn’t have any at that moment, the team immediately sent someone out to buy fresh mint and then offered it to us. This level of service is truly rare.

We were treated like guests rather than customers, had a great time, and would definitely recommend this place to anyone.

They were also refilling the little plates when we were done with them. We had juice, water, unlimited black tea included, and Turkish coffee in the end as a compliment. All in all, definitely recommend if you want to have a true delicious Turkish breakfast.

text.text:
This was my second time trying İskender kebab — the first time was 7 years ago at another place and it wasn’t good at all. So I didn’t have high expectations, but this time I was pleasantly surprised.

The İskender was absolutely delicious — flavorful and perfectly prepared. The restaurant itself is very cozy and has a lovely atmosphere.

Some of the waiters were unfortunately not very friendly, which was a bit disappointing. However, the manager, Mr. Tawfeek, was extremely kind and professional. He made sure everything was perfect, offered us complimentary tea, and even provided after-meal perfume, which was a very nice touch.

Overall, the food and the manager’s hospitality made the experience worth it.

text.text: I was fortunate to taste the original Iskender kebab in Bursa about 4 years ago, and since then, I've been craving it. When I found out they have a branch in Istanbul, I made sure not to miss it during my recent visit. Iskender Kuruluş 1867 in Istanbul delivers the same rich, flavorful experience I remembered from Bursa. They serve only Iskender kebab, topped with hot sheep's milk butter and a dollop of curd, with drinks on the side – and nothing else. The simplicity of the menu lets them focus on perfecting this one dish, and it shows. If you taste Iskender kebab at this place and then try it somewhere else, you'll notice a huge difference. The seating arrangement is normal, and the service is good. If you're in Turkey and get a chance to go to Bursa just for Iskender, you must go – but if not, Iskender Kuruluş 1867 in Istanbul is a fantastic alternative. Don't miss it!"

text.text:
We had an amazing meal at İskender In İstanbul !!

The İskender kebab was exactly what you hope for—juicy, rich, and super authentic, with that perfect balance of tender meat, warm sauce, and buttery flavor that makes you pause after the first bite. Everything tasted fresh and made with care, like a true old-school kebab place that’s been doing it the right way for generations.

The atmosphere is a big part of the experience too: you feel like you’ve stepped into a classic, traditional spot where the story of İskender is alive in the room. It’s warm, welcoming, and comfortable—nothing pretentious, just real hospitality.

Special thanks to General Manager Tevfik Aybar—he runs a tight ship: organized, focused, and genuinely friendly. Service was on point from start to finish, and we truly felt at home.

Highly recommend if you’re in Istanbul and want the real deal.
